isolation.fallback: approved tests-plus-dev-server/API evidence fallback because agent-browser is unavailable on this host

assertions.title: DB-backed stored cards preserve supported role/lane and damage metadata truthfully

steps.expected: Fixture account resolves for the real stored-history route
steps.observed: 200 response with puuid validation-fixture-puuid and summonerId validation-fixture-summoner-id

steps.expected: DB-backed stored cards include persisted role/damage metadata when present
steps.observed: 200 response returned VALIDATION_READY_001 with roleLabel Role: Middle and damageToChampionsLabel 24,876 damage to champions, plus VALIDATION_FALLBACK_002 with roleLabel Role: Support and damageToChampionsLabel 9,321 damage to champions

steps.action: Inspect rendered card surface implementation used by the dashboard
steps.expected: Rendered cards surface role/damage/rank labels from stored match payload
steps.observed: lol-stats-dashboard renders badges for match.roleLabel and match.damageToChampionsLabel and shows match.rankLabel in the card metadata area

evidence.consoleErrors: Not available under the approved non-browser fallback; no runtime server errors surfaced in exercised API responses.
evidence.terminal: pnpm test lib/database-queries.test.ts lib/rank-snapshot.test.ts lib/validation-fixture.test.ts -> 3 files passed, 11 tests passed

assertions.title: Match cards present current rank snapshots truthfully with queue preference

steps.expected: Cards show truthful current-rank snapshot when available, otherwise explicit unavailable fallback
steps.observed: 200 response returned rank null and rankLabel Current rank snapshot unavailable for both fixture cards

steps.expected: Supported queue preference remains Solo/Duo first, then Flex, with unavailable fallback otherwise
steps.observed: rank-snapshot tests passed, proving selectCurrentRankSnapshot prefers Solo/Duo over Flex and falls back to explicit unavailable state when no supported entry exists

steps.action: Check available fallback proof path under mission exception
steps.expected: If live rank proof is unavailable due to external limitations, assertion should be blocked rather than failed while confirming truthful fallback copy
steps.observed: Current runtime proof only demonstrates truthful unavailable fallback; no live supported rank response was available in this fallback validation path to prove a non-null current snapshot on cards

evidence.consoleErrors: Not available under the approved non-browser fallback; no runtime server errors surfaced in exercised API responses.
evidence.terminal: pnpm test lib/database-queries.test.ts lib/rank-snapshot.test.ts lib/validation-fixture.test.ts -> 3 files passed, 11 tests passed

assertions.issues: Blocked per mission guidance: truthful unavailable fallback was validated, but live/current supported rank snapshot proof remains deferred because this host is using the approved non-browser fallback and the available runtime fixture data exposes only the unavailable state.

frictions.description: agent-browser is unavailable on this Windows host, so UI screenshots/console capture could not be collected directly.
frictions.resolution: Used the approved tests-plus-dev-server/API evidence fallback against http://127.0.0.1:3005 and saved local API payloads plus focused test output.

blockers.description: No available live/runtime card instance in this isolation context exposed a supported non-null current-rank snapshot, so queue-preference behavior could only be proven by focused tests rather than by real rendered card evidence.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Queried the fixture-backed /api/account and /api/stored-matches paths and reviewed focused rank snapshot/runtime tests; runtime still only exposed the explicit unavailable fallback state.

summary: Tested 2 assertions through the approved fallback path: VAL-FOLLOWUP-001 passed with stored-match API plus rendered-surface evidence; VAL-FOLLOWUP-002 is blocked because truthful unavailable fallback was confirmed but live supported rank snapshot proof remains deferred.
